commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Needs contributor/live proof plus maintainer review of the visibility-gating fix before merge; automation cannot supply the missing external proof.; Needs attention: The diff can bypass Telegram group context visibility by moving hydrated reply media paths into raw prompt context.; Review finding: [P1] Gate hydrated refs on visible reply-chain entries — extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts:1158 (sha=54d917c19954e2f56d261421c46d290a94c3f45d)
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes Telegram prompt-context projection so an already-downloaded reply-chain media path can replace the cached `telegram:file/...` value in `media_ref`, with a focused bot test update.

PR surface: Source +18, Tests +1. Total +19 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Source-reproducible, but not live-reproduced here: current main emits the cached `telegram:file/...` `media_ref`, and the PR test shows the intended hydrated reply-media path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor/live proof plus maintainer review of the visibility-gating fix before merge; automation cannot supply the missing external proof.

target.title: fix(telegram): expose hydrated media refs in prompt context
target.body:
## Summary

Closes #87401.

Telegram reply-image context could reach the agent as `telegram:file/<file_id>`, which the image tool cannot inspect. This keeps the Telegram file id as plugin-internal metadata, but uses the already-hydrated reply-chain media path for model-facing structured context when that path exists.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: Telegram photo replies no longer expose an unsupported `telegram:file/<file_id>` reference as the model-facing `media_ref` when the reply-chain image was already downloaded for the current Telegram message.

Real environment tested: Telegram channel behavior from #87401: OpenClaw v2026.5.18, Telegram plugin enabled, headless Ubuntu 24.04 host. The reported Telegram photo was downloaded into `media/inbound/`, but conversation context still exposed `telegram:file/...` to the image tool. This PR verifies the same Telegram handler/media path on PR head `54d917c199`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts -t 'hydrates bot reply targets cached from earlier Telegram replies'`; `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts`; `git diff --check origin/main`.

Evidence after fix: Terminal capture from the focused Telegram handler run showed the after-fix context payload for reply-target message `101`: `payload.ReplyChain[1].mediaPath` was a hydrated local media path, `UntrustedStructuredContext.messages[101].media_ref` matched that hydrated media path, and `UntrustedStructuredContext.messages[101].media_ref !== "telegram:file/generated-photo-1"`. The same run ended with `Test Files 1 passed (1)` and `Tests 1 passed | 71 skipped (72)`.

Observed result after fix: Agent-facing Telegram structured context receives an image-tool-readable local media path for the hydrated reply image. The unsupported `telegram:file/generated-photo-1` value remains only as internal reply-chain metadata, not the model-facing `media_ref`.

What was not tested: Broader Telegram flows and the full repository suite were not rerun for this narrow PR.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts -t 'hydrates bot reply targets cached from earlier Telegram replies'`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main`

## Risk

- User-visible behavior changes only for Telegram reply-image context exposed to the agent.
- No config, migration, auth, secret, or network behavior changes.
- The override is limited to existing reply-chain entries that already have a hydrated media path; otherwise the cached `mediaRef` fallback is unchanged.
target.author: monkeyleet

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
